name: Wheat bread (food medium)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Proximate; range 33.2-42.2 g (n=9).

components.name: starch
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source; hydrolysed to glucose by amylases before/at fermentation.

components.name: total dietary fiber
components.evidence: Arabinoxylan/cellulose/beta-glucan; largely non-fermentable by most single strains.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Mostly gluten; amino-acid breakdown listed individually below.

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: Fatty-acid breakdown below.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose from fermentation/amylolysis; mM assuming 100 g ~ 100 mL, MW 180.16.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Most abundant free sugar; MW 180.16.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: From starch hydrolysis; MW 342.30.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Not detected (consumed during fermentation).

components.name: galactose
components.evidence: Not detected.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Sum reference for the free sugars above.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Standard yeast-leavened bread 97-108 mg/kg dry basis (Fortune J. sourdough characterization); Novotni/Aponte reviews
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record. Predominant organic acid. Value shown = standard yeast-leavened wheat bread (~0.01 g/100g fresh). Sourdough wheat bread is 20-70x higher: 0.5-0.7 g/100g (5.45-7.08 mg/g). MW 90.08.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: Sourdough survey (Chemical/Nutritional Characterization of Sourdoughs, PMC12385715): ~0.64 mg/g where detected
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record. Trace in straight-dough bread; up to ~0.06 g/100g in sourdough. Value = typical low-end; MW 60.05.

components.name: propionic acid
components.source: Calcium propionate is a standard commercial bread preservative, typical use 0.1-0.3 g/100g flour (E282; FDA GRAS 21 CFR 184.1221)
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record. Present in most commercial (non-sourdough) wheat bread as added mold inhibitor; absent from label-clean/sourdough loaves. MW 74.08.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record. Yeast produces low levels; present but not quantified for generic bread.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.source: USDA FDC 172687 (Bread, wheat) scaled 10.7/13.0 to untoasted protein basis
components.evidence: Dominant amino acid (gluten glutamine/glutamate). AA profile derived from same food (wheat bread) rescaled to this loaf's 10.7 g protein.

components.name: L-proline
components.source: USDA FDC 172687 scaled to 10.7 g protein
components.evidence: High proline is a gluten signature.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: D-Serine in FooDB record is the L-form here.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Essential AA.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Essential AA; not in FooDB record.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Essential AA; not in FooDB record.

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: Essential AA.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: Essential AA.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Limiting essential AA in wheat.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Reported as cystine (2x cysteine).

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Essential AA; co-limiting in wheat.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Essential AA; not in FooDB record.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: From added salt (NaCl); major electrolyte, previously unquantified.

components.name: phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: Phosphorus 129 mg; largely as phytate/phosphate.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Includes fortification/propionate salt.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Enrichment iron; FooDB Fe2+/Fe3+ entries collapse to this.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Wheat is a notable Se source.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Not in USDA panel; ~730 mg/100g inferred from salt but left null (unmeasured).

components.name: thiamine (B1)
components.evidence: Enriched.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Enriched.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Enriched.

components.name: folate (B9, total)
components.evidence: 20 µg folic acid + 65 µg food folate; 99 µg DFE.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Wheat bread biotin roughly 1-6 µg/100g (McCance & Widdowson); left null (unquantified in cited source).

components.name: vitamin C
components.evidence: Trace; ascorbate is a common dough improver additive.

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.evidence: Dominant tocopherol in wheat lipids (> alpha).

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: Phytomenadione in FooDB record.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Wheat is betaine-rich; usable osmoprotectant/methyl donor.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Trace; lutein+zeaxanthin 44 µg.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Major saturated FA.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Major MUFA.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Most abundant fatty acid overall.

components.name: ethanol
components.source: USDA FDC 172686 lists 0.0 (baked off); fresh crumb retains ~0.1-0.4 g/100g
components.evidence: Residual yeast-fermentation ethanol, mostly evaporated on baking; effectively trace.

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: Animal sterol; USDA measures 0 mg in wheat bread. FooDB predicted/implausible for a plant/cereal food.

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: Vitamin B12 = 0 µg (USDA); cereals contain no B12. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed vitamin A = 0 µg (USDA); no animal-derived retinol in plain wheat bread.

remove.name: Lactose C12H22O11
remove.reason: Lactose = 0 g (USDA); no dairy sugar in basic wheat bread (only in milk breads).

remove.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
remove.reason: Marine omega-3 (20:5 n-3); USDA lists 0.004 g trace, biologically implausible as a bread nutrient. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a defined growth nutrient of bread.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion trace; only nutritionally relevant via B12, which is absent. Non-quantified FooDB trace.

remove.name: Nonanoate
remove.reason: C9:0 odd-chain FA not measured in USDA bread panel; FooDB predicted trace, not a real lipid component.

remove.name: Heptanoate
remove.reason: C7:0 odd-chain FA absent from USDA panel; FooDB predicted trace.

missing_added: lactic acid (dominant organic acid, was absent)
quantitative free sugars (glucose 1.66, fructose 2.33, maltose 1.74 g/100g)
starch amount (36.3 g/100g, dominant carbon source)
full amino-acid amounts incl. Phe, Val, Trp (missing from record)
sodium 473 mg/100g and full quantitative mineral panel
quantitative vitamin panel (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6, folate, E, K)
sources: USDA FDC 172687 (Bread, wheat, toasted) - amino-acid profile, scaled to 10.7 g protein
Aponte et al. 2022, LAB & yeasts in sourdough, PMC9524358 (organic-acid qualitative)
Chemical & Nutritional Characterization of Sourdoughs, PMC12385715 (lactic 5.45-7.08 mg/g; acetic 0.64 mg/g)
Characteristics of new sourdough using LAB and wild yeast (Fortune Journals) - straight-dough lactic 97-108 mg/kg dry
FDA 21 CFR 184.1221 - calcium propionate GRAS preservative use levels
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe on this medium are STARCH (36.3 g/100g, needs amylolysis) plus free glucose+fructose+maltose (~5.7 g/100g total). Organic acids - the main FooDB omission - are modest in standard yeast-leavened wheat bread (lactic ~0.01 g/100g) but rise 20-70x in sourdough (0.5-0.7 g/100g); propionic acid is present as an added preservative in most commercial loaves. Amino-acid pattern is gluten-typed (glutamate and proline dominant, lysine/methionine limiting).
